exemplar.title: M25 #1: Do historians and human scientists have an ethical obligation to follow the directive: "do not ignore contradictory evidence"? Discuss with reference to history and the human sciences.

detailedExemplarData.title: M25 #1: Do historians and human scientists have an ethical obligation to follow the directive: "do not ignore contradictory evidence"? Discuss with reference to history and the human sciences.

strengths: Sustained, clear focus on the prescribed title throughout the essay
Coherent, well-supported arguments using specific, discipline-specific examples
Effective evaluation of alternative perspectives and reflection on broader implications
weaknesses: Occasional descriptive passages that could benefit from deeper analytical probing (e.g., American Civil War example)
Structural and citation inconsistencies (abrupt transitions, incomplete bibliographic details)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Given the examinerâs total of 9 and the strand-level evidence, the single TOK Essay criterion is awarded 9 out of 10. The essay demonstrates a sustained focus on the title, consistently linking the discussion to both history and the human sciences. Arguments are developed through coherent lines of reasoning and underpinned by well-chosen examples such as colonial historiography, Holocaust denial, the Charcot/Freud debate, and the DSM reclassification of homosexuality. There is clear awareness and evaluation of different points of view, and the implications of ignoring contradictory evidence are thoughtfully explored. Minor lapses in deeper analytical depth in a few case studies, occasional underdeveloped transitions, and citation inconsistencies account for withholding the top mark, but overall the work firmly resides in the 9â10 band.

annotations.comment: The American Civil War case is well chosen but the analysis remains descriptive. It requires deeper examination of how newly integrated evidence reshaped prevailing interpretations.
Acknowledging practical constraints like limited data access demonstrates a nuanced understanding of real-world research challenges in both disciplines.
The clear ordering of bibliography entries supports reader navigation and indicates careful organization of the research foundation.
The discussion of evidence-inclusion thresholds raises an important KQ but lacks concrete suggestions for operationalising this threshold in practice.
The Charcot vs. Freud case is an excellent historical example illustrating how contradictory evidence prompted theoretical evolution, demonstrating strong use of discipline-specific detail.
The reference lacks consistent formatting and publisher details. Ensure all citations include full bibliographic information for transparency and verification.
The DSM homosexuality example powerfully demonstrates the ethical imperative of reconsidering evidence, showing clear awareness of the human sciencesâ social impact.
The comparative analysis of objectivity versus subjectivity in history and human sciences highlights shared epistemological challenges, reinforcing the essayâs TOK focus on ways of knowing.

The point about antidepressant controversies is too generic. Including a specific clinical trial or data set would concretely demonstrate how institutional pressures shape publication of contradictory findings.

The example contrasting 19th-century imperial narratives with indigenous oral histories effectively illustrates how omitting contradictory evidence sustains bias, demonstrating clear historical awareness.

The title clearly states the prescribed question and scope, immediately orienting the reader to the ethical injunction and the two AOKs under discussion, which establishes a solid foundation for focused analysis.
The discussion of Holocaust denial vividly underscores the ethical stakes of ignoring evidence, effectively linking historical revisionism to real-world consequences for public memory.

The definition of âcontradictory evidenceâ is presented too simplistically. It would benefit from nuance regarding source reliability, contextual bias, and criteria for evaluating conflicting testimonies.
The transition from the introduction to the first body paragraph is abrupt. A clearer signposting sentence is needed to connect the thesis to the discussion of historical methodology.
The bibliography heading is clear, but in-text citations should correspond precisely to entries; ensure superscript markers match full reference details.
The footnote omits volume and page numbers. Standardise citation style by including all relevant details, such as journal volume, issue, and page range.
This paragraph ends mid-argument, leaving the point about contested histories underdeveloped. It needs completion to sustain the line of reasoning.
The shift to human sciences is introduced too abruptly. A more explicit bridge explaining why methodological parallels matter would enhance coherence between sections.
This sentence fragment introduces human sciences challenges but is left incomplete. Expand to fully articulate the types of methodological obstacles encountered.
The inclusion of feminist critiques in the bibliography reflects interdisciplinary insight, reinforcing the essayâs commitment to multiple perspectives.
The bibliography relies heavily on older sources. Integrating more recent scholarship would demonstrate engagement with current TOK and disciplinary debates.
Entries lack digital identifiers (DOIs or URLs). Including electronic access information would improve source traceability and academic rigour.
The Freud citation omits context such as original publication location and page range. Adding this information ensures completeness and aids verification.

The essay does not
reach a standard
described by levels
1â5 or is not a
response to one of
the prescribed titles
on the list for the
current session.
The discussion is weakly connected to the title. While there may be links to the areas of knowledge, any relevant points are descriptive or consist only of unsupported assertions.

The discussion is connected to the title and makes superficial or limited links to areas of knowledge. The discussion is largely descriptive. Limited arguments are offered but they are unclear and are not supported by effective examples.

The discussion
is focused on
the title and is
developed with
some links to
areas of
knowledge.
Arguments are
offered and are
supported by
examples.
There is some
awareness of
different points
of view.

The discussion is focused on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and supported by examples. There is awareness and some evaluation of different points of view.

The discussion has a sustained focus on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and effectively supported by specific examples. The implications of arguments are considered. There is clear awareness and evaluation of different points of view.
